texto: Love is like air, babe. It's there all the time, and you don't even think about it,or you take it for granted. Then all of a sudden, you need it, or you can't breathe. You inhale, and for the first time you're aware it's keeping you alive. You feel it brush your skin all the time. Sometimes it's warm and other times it's cool, but it's there surrounding you, feeding you, holding you. When you finally realize it's love, you become vulnerable. With that new fear of not having air, you subconsciously allow someone special to breathe life for you.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Debra Kayn: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Love is like air, babe. It's there all the time, and you don't even think about it,or you take it…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, bikers, contemporary-romance pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de contemporary-romance.
